# VER-QUIC-INT-0004 - QUIC Interop Post-Handshake Stream Open/Accept Verification

## Scope

Verify the managed child-process `post-handshake-stream` testcase, the handshake-completion gate, the client-side first-stream open, the server-side first-stream accept, and the honesty of unsupported transfer and retry cases.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-INT-0011

## Verification Method

Focused requirement-home tests, build execution, and artifact inspection.

## Preconditions

- REQ-QUIC-API-0002, REQ-QUIC-INT-0007, REQ-QUIC-INT-0008, and REQ-QUIC-CRT-0121 remain green.
- The harness can dispatch `post-handshake-stream` through the managed child-process path.
- Loopback UDP sockets are available in the test environment.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the new post-handshake-stream requirement-home test and confirm the client opens the first application stream after connect completion while the server accepts it after accept completion.
- Run the existing handshake/public guard tests and the CRT client 1-RTT readiness proof to keep the prerequisite floor green.
- Run the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep.
- Render and validate the SpecTrace JSON and Markdown artifacts after any canonical JSON updates.

## Expected Result

The child-process harness proves the first application-stream open/accept pair after handshake completion while transfer and retry remain `127`.

## Status

Planned; this verification slice records the proof contract for the smaller prerequisite beneath the transfer-owned slice.

## Deferred Coverage

Byte delivery, EOF, transfer enablement, retry enablement, multi-stream generalization, broader 1-RTT data-path ownership, trust-store policy widening, 0-RTT, and key update remain intentionally deferred.
